distribution_plot.caption: Distribution of 5 categories across 5,630 customers. Horizontal bar chart chosen for readability at this category count.

stacked_churn_plot.caption: Each bar spans 100% of customers inside that category. Red = churn rate. Orange dashed line = overall retention baseline — any category whose red segment extends PAST this line churns more than average (a hotspot).

chi_squared.narration: χ² = 288.60 on 4 df. Highly significant (p < 0.0001). Tests whether the category proportions differ between churn and retained.

cramers_v.narration: Cramér's V = 0.226 — moderate association with churn.

narrative.distribution: 'preferred_order_cat' has 5 category/ies across 5,630 customers. Most common: 'Mobile' (36.9%). Least common: 'Others' (4.7%).
narrative.churn_rates: Overall churn rate = 16.8%. Highest-churn category: 'Mobile' at 27.4% (1.63× the baseline). Lowest-churn category: 'Grocery' at 4.9% (0.29× the baseline). Hotspot categories (churn rate > 1.5× baseline): Mobile. Strong candidates for a binary flag feature in Stage 3.2.
narrative.association: χ² = 288.60 on 4 df. Highly significant (p < 0.0001). Tests whether the category proportions differ between churn and retained. Cramér's V = 0.226 — moderate association with churn.

recommendation.rationale: Moderate association (Cramér's V = 0.226). One-hot is sufficient; tree models will pick up the interactions.
engineering_ideas: Create binary flag(s) for hotspot categories: Mobile.
